question: What happens if the primary on-call does not acknowledge an alert?
expected_answer_notes: Answer should mention escalation to secondary on-call and then engineering manager.

retrieved_docs.title: Engineering On-Call Expectations
retrieved_docs.text: Primary on-call engineers must acknowledge pager alerts within 10 minutes and begin triage within 15 minutes. If no acknowledgment occurs, the alert escalates automatically to the secondary on-call engineer and then to the engineering manager.

retrieved_docs.title: Expense Reimbursement Rules
retrieved_docs.text: Meals under 40 USD do not require pre-approval. Hotel bookings above 180 USD per night require director approval unless travel occurs during a peak event period. Expense claims must include receipts and be submitted within 30 days of purchase.

retrieved_docs.title: Vendor Review Requirements
retrieved_docs.text: New vendors handling customer data must complete a security questionnaire, sign a data processing agreement, and provide evidence of encryption at rest and in transit. Annual renewals require reassessment if risk exposure changes.

final_prompt:
You are a concise QA assistant for eval exercises. Answer only from the retrieved context. If the context does not support the answer, say that clearly.
Retrieved context:
- [doc-009] Engineering On-Call Expectations (engineering): Primary on-call engineers must acknowledge pager alerts within 10 minutes and begin triage within 15 minutes. If no acknowledgment occurs, the alert escalates automatically to the secondary on-call engineer and then to the engineering manager.
- [doc-003] Expense Reimbursement Rules (finance): Meals under 40 USD do not require pre-approval. Hotel bookings above 180 USD per night require director approval unless travel occurs during a peak event period. Expense claims must include receipts and be submitted within 30 days of purchase.
- [doc-012] Vendor Review Requirements (procurement): New vendors handling customer data must complete a security questionnaire, sign a data processing agreement, and provide evidence of encryption at rest and in transit. Annual renewals require reassessment if risk exposure changes.
Question: What happens if the primary on-call does not acknowledge an alert?
Answer:
answer: If the primary on-call does not acknowledge an alert, it escalates automatically to the secondary on-call engineer and then to the engineering manager.
